title: Frontend admin gating reliability for creator principal

requirements.summary: Adjust frontend admin gating to rely on updated backend admin checks and prevent false "Access Denied" blocking for the creator on the Admin Dashboard (including Admin Support lookups).
acceptanceCriteria: Logging in as the creator principal allows navigation to the Admin Dashboard and all tabs (including Support) without being blocked by AdminGate.
The Admin Support lookup flow functions normally for creator/admin users (submits a principal ID, shows either a diagnostic report or a clear error message in English).
User-facing text introduced or modified as part of this change is in English.

file_operations.description: Prevent premature "Access Denied" rendering when authentication/actor state is not ready (e.g., user not logged in yet, or admin status query not yet enabled). Add an authenticated-state-aware gate that (1) prompts login when unauthenticated, (2) shows a loading state while admin status is being resolved, and (3) only shows "Access Denied" once admin status has definitively resolved to false.

Harden the admin-status query behavior to improve reliability after backend changes: avoid treating transient actor/identity initialization states as a definitive non-admin result, ensure the query does not endlessly retry on authorization traps, and ensure the hook refetches appropriately after identity changes (without changing non-admin semantics).

Align Admin navigation link visibility with the improved admin gating behavior so the creator/admin can consistently see and navigate to the Admin route after login (avoid hiding the link due to transient unresolved admin status).
